cases.title: NIST SD19 handwriting sample form
cases.failure_mode: Handwriting mixed with printed form structure
cases.observation: PSM 6 emphasizes the handwritten constitutional passage while PSM 11 surfaces more printed labels and isolated number rows; neither plain-text output preserves the form as fields.

cases.title: 1930 Census population schedule
cases.source_status: U.S. National Archives educational PDF derivative
cases.failure_mode: Handwriting inside a dense row and column grid
cases.observation: Both outputs contain fragments, but the plain text does not retain row identity or column attachment, which are necessary to interpret a census record.

cases.title: Alexander Graham Bell notebook page
cases.source_status: Library of Congress image; see source page for rights information
cases.failure_mode: Faded historical handwriting and irregular reading order
cases.observation: Both profiles produce many plausible-looking fragments, illustrating why fluent output still requires line-by-line comparison with the source.

cases.title: IRS Form W-4
cases.source_status: U.S. federal government public document derivative
cases.failure_mode: Printed form with checkboxes, fields, and multi-column semantics
cases.observation: Large amounts of text are surfaced, but spelling substitutions appear and plain text does not preserve checkbox state or reliable label-to-field relationships.
